title: Prompt Injection
description: Prompt Injection involves crafting malicious inputs to manipulate LLM behavior, override instructions, or exfiltrate data.
remediation: Sanitize and validate all user inputs. Use structured prompts with clear delimiters. Implement input guardrails.

title: Sensitive Information Disclosure
description: LLMs may inadvertently disclose sensitive data, passwords, API keys, or PII from their training data or context window.
remediation: Remove all sensitive data from system prompts. Implement output filtering to redact secrets. Use secret managers.

title: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
description: Third-party integrations, plugins, or model providers can introduce supply chain risks.
remediation: Audit all third-party integrations. Pin dependency versions. Monitor for security advisories.

title: Model Denial of Service
description: Adversaries can cause resource exhaustion through complex queries, infinite loops, or resource-intensive prompts.
remediation: Implement token limits, rate limiting, and circuit breakers. Monitor resource consumption.

title: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
description: Inadequate output validation may allow the model to generate misleading, harmful, or malicious content.
remediation: Implement robust output validation and content filtering. Use secondary models for output review.

title: Excessive Agency
description: LLM agents with overly broad permissions can take harmful autonomous actions — file deletion, SQL drops, shell commands — without proper authorization.
remediation: Apply principle of least privilege. Require human-in-the-loop for destructive operations. Implement tool allowlists.

title: System Prompt Leakage
description: System prompts containing business logic, secrets, or sensitive context can be extracted by adversarial users.
remediation: Design system prompts defensively. Do not embed secrets. Use prompt injection detection.

title: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
description: Attackers may manipulate vector stores or embeddings to alter RAG outputs and influence agent decisions.
remediation: Implement access controls on vector stores. Validate retrieved content before including in prompts.

title: Misinformation
description: LLMs may generate plausible but factually incorrect outputs that users trust without verification.
remediation: Implement RAG with verified sources. Add disclaimers. Use output verification mechanisms.

title: Unbounded Consumption
description: Excessive token consumption or tool calls can lead to significant financial and performance costs.
remediation: Set hard limits on token usage and tool call counts per session. Implement cost monitoring and alerts.
